metadata.name: Dreadhorde Invasion — cast and resolve; amass Zombies upkeep trigger (engine gap)
metadata.description: p1 casts Dreadhorde Invasion ({1}{B}, Enchantment). Both players pass priority. The spell resolves and Dreadhorde Invasion enters p1's battlefield. The script validates the cast→resolve flow and confirms the enchantment is on the battlefield. Dreadhorde Invasion has an upkeep triggered ability: 'At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 1 life and amass Zombies 1.' Per CR 701.47a, amass Zombies 1 means: if you don't control an Army, create a 0/0 black Zombie Army creature token, then put 1 +1/+1 counter on it (making it a 1/1). On p1's second upkeep, the same trigger fires and the existing Army (now 1/1) receives a second +1/+1 counter (making it a 2/2). p1 loses 1 life per trigger. The upkeep trigger lifecycle is an engine gap in the replay harness (see disputes); it is validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/amass.rs.
metadata.generation_notes: Dreadhorde Invasion oracle text: 'At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 1 life and amass Zombies 1. (Put a +1/+1 counter on an Army you control. It's also a Zombie. If you don't control an Army, create a 0/0 black Zombie Army creature token first.) Whenever a Zombie token you control with power 6 or greater attacks, it gains lifelink until end of turn.' Cost: {1}{B} = generic:1, black:1. Card definition (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/dreadhorde_invasion.rs): AbilityDefinition::Triggered { trigger_condition: TriggerCondition::AtBeginningOfYourUpkeep, effect: Effect::Sequence([LoseLife { player: Controller, amount: Fixed(1) }, Amass { subtype: 'Zombie', count: Fixed(1) }]) }. CR 701.47a (amass Zombies N): if controller has no Army, create a 0/0 black Zombie Army creature token, then choose an Army and put N +1/+1 counters on it. CR 701.47b: Amassed event fires even if some actions were impossible. ENGINE GAP: The replay harness does not drive generic AtBeginningOfYourUpkeep triggered abilities from card definitions. The upkeep_actions() function in rules/turn_actions.rs only queues specific keyword ability triggers (Suspend, Vanishing, Fading, Echo, CumulativeUpkeep). It does NOT scan card definitions for AtBeginningOfYourUpkeep triggers and queue them as PendingTrigger { kind: PendingTriggerKind::Normal }. Additionally, check_triggers() in rules/abilities.rs handles event-driven triggers only; it does NOT respond to StepChanged { step: Upkeep } to fire card-definition upkeep triggers. The full amass lifecycle (trigger fires, LoseLife -1, token created 0/0, +1/+1 counter applied → 1/1 on upkeep 1; existing Army gets +1 counter → 2/2 on upkeep 2) is validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/amass.rs. The SECOND ability ('Whenever a Zombie token with power 6+ attacks, it gains lifelink until end of turn') is not implemented in the card definition (deferred — no DSL support for attack trigger with token-type filter + power threshold). Script scope: cast Dreadhorde Invasion → resolve → assert on battlefield. The informational section below documents the two-upkeep lifecycle per CR 701.47a/b for future reference when the harness gap is fixed.

disputes.description: ENGINE GAP (CR 603.2 + CR 701.47a): Dreadhorde Invasion's upkeep triggered ability ('At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 1 life and amass Zombies 1') does not fire through the replay harness. Two gaps exist: (1) upkeep_actions() in rules/turn_actions.rs only queues Suspend/Vanishing/Fading/Echo/CumulativeUpkeep triggers, not generic AtBeginningOfYourUpkeep card-definition triggers. (2) check_triggers() in rules/abilities.rs is event-driven and does not respond to StepChanged { step: Upkeep } to fire card-definition upkeep triggers. Per CR 603.2, the trigger should fire at the beginning of p1's upkeep step, putting it on the stack; after both players pass, it resolves: p1 loses 1 life and amass Zombies 1 fires (CR 701.47a: create 0/0 black Zombie Army token if none, then put 1 counter on it, making it 1/1). On the second upkeep, the Army would receive a second counter (2/2) and p1 would be at 38 life. Fix: add a general AtBeginningOfYourUpkeep pass to upkeep_actions() that scans battlefield permanents controlled by the active player, finds AbilityDefinition::Triggered { trigger_condition: AtBeginningOfYourUpkeep } entries, and queues them as PendingTrigger { kind: Normal, ability_index: <index> }. The amass effect itself is correctly implemented and tested in crates/engine/tests/amass.rs (7 tests, CR 701.47a-d).

script.step_note: p1 casts Dreadhorde Invasion ({1}{B}) from hand. Both players pass priority. The spell resolves and the enchantment enters p1's battlefield.

actions.description: Initial: Dreadhorde Invasion in p1 hand; battlefield empty; stack empty; p1 at 40 life
actions.note: Dreadhorde Invasion ({1}{B} Enchantment, Amass Zombies upkeep trigger) is in p1's hand. p1 has {1}{B} (colorless:1, black:1) in mana pool ready to cast.
CR 601.2: p1 casts Dreadhorde Invasion; {1}{B} paid from pool (colorless:1, black:1). The enchantment has no targets. It moves from hand to stack.

actions.description: After cast: Dreadhorde Invasion on stack (count 1); p1 hand empty
actions.note: CR 601.2: Dreadhorde Invasion is on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Dreadhorde Invasion resolves (CR 608.2).

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Dreadhorde Invasion is a permanent spell; it resolves by entering the battlefield under p1's control. The upkeep trigger (CR 603.2) will fire at the beginning of p1's next upkeep — not now.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Enchantment spell resolves and enters the battlefield as a permanent. Its upkeep triggered ability ('At the beginning of your upkeep, lose 1 life and amass Zombies 1') fires at the start of p1's upkeep step, not at ETB time.

actions.description: Final: Dreadhorde Invasion on p1 battlefield; stack empty; both players at 40 life; no tokens yet
actions.note: Dreadhorde Invasion is now a permanent on p1's battlefield. No life has been lost and no Army token has been created yet — those happen when the upkeep trigger fires (engine gap — see disputes).

script.step_note: INFORMATIONAL — Dreadhorde Invasion upkeep trigger lifecycle documentation (CR 701.47a). These stack_resolve actions document correct rules behavior but are NOT executed by the harness due to the engine gap (see disputes). Validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/amass.rs.

resolution.note: CR 118.2: p1 loses 1 life as part of the upkeep trigger's Sequence effect. p1 goes from 40 to 39 life.

CR 701.47a: p1 controls no Army. Create a 0/0 black Zombie Army creature token. Choose the new Army and put 1 +1/+1 counter on it, making it a 1/1 Zombie Army token. CR 701.47b: Amassed event is emitted.

actions.note: INFORMATIONAL (engine gap): First upkeep trigger fires; p1 loses 1 life (39 life) and amasses Zombies 1. No Army exists — a 0/0 black Zombie Army token is created, then 1 +1/+1 counter placed on it (now 1/1). After this upkeep p1 should have 39 life and a 1/1 Zombie Army on the battlefield.

resolution.note: CR 118.2: p1 loses 1 more life. p1 goes from 39 to 38 life.
CR 701.47a: p1 controls an Army (the 1/1 Zombie Army token from upkeep 1). p1 chooses it and puts 1 +1/+1 counter on it (now 2/2). No new token is created. CR 701.47b: Amassed event emitted.

actions.note: INFORMATIONAL (engine gap): Second upkeep trigger fires; p1 loses 1 more life (38 life) and amasses Zombies 1 again. Army already exists (1/1 from upkeep 1) — 1 counter added, now a 2/2 Zombie Army. After upkeep 2: p1 at 38 life, Zombie Army is 2/2 with 2 +1/+1 counters.
